raw_response:
The β-oxidation process involves breaking down long chains of fatty acids into acetyl-CoA units via repeated cycles of oxidation reactions. For odd-chain fatty acids, after complete degradation, one molecule of propionyl-CoA remains due to the presence of three carbon atoms left over during the final cycle of β-oxidation. Therefore, the correct answer would be:
\boxed{B} The β-oxidation of odd-chain fatty acids indeed produces propionyl-CoA as a result of the remaining two-carbon fragment after full degradation, making \boxed{B} the correct response. No other metabolite listed matches the expected outcome specifically for odd-chain fatty acids post-β-oxidation.
